examples.title: Phong Lighting Calculation
examples.description: Implementation of Phong illumination model for a single point light source
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the complete Phong model. It normalizes all vectors, computes reflection vector R, then calculates ambient, diffuse, and specular components using the Phong equation.

examples.title: Reflection Vector Calculation
examples.description: Computes reflection direction using surface normal
examples.explanation: The reflection vector R is calculated using the formula R = 2(N·L)N - L, which is derived from the law of reflection. The result is the direction in which light would bounce off the surface.

examples.title: Multiple Light Sources Phong Model
examples.description: Extends Phong model to handle multiple light sources
examples.explanation: This extends the basic Phong model to handle multiple light sources by summing the illumination contribution from each light. Each light can have different intensity and color.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m) where m = number of lights
